questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy?

options.text: To sell Notion licenses to businesses
options.explanation: The Academy is an educational program, not a sales channel.

options.text: To help users learn Notion, build workflows, and earn badges
options.explanation: The article states that Notion Academy turns curiosity into capability, offering learning paths and badges.

options.text: To provide live technical support for account issues
options.explanation: Support is handled through the Help Center, not the Academy.

options.text: To host third‑party advertisements within Notion pages
options.explanation: Advertising is not mentioned as a function of Notion Academy.

questions.question: What can you earn by completing a Notion Academy learning path?

options.text: A discount code for Notion subscriptions
options.explanation: Discount codes are not listed as a reward for completing paths.

options.text: A Notion badge that validates your skill
options.explanation: The article highlights that learners earn badges after finishing paths.
Physical merchandise is not mentioned as a benefit.
Career advancement depends on the employer, not on the Academy badge.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a featured lesson in the article?

options.explanation: Custom Agents is explicitly listed as a featured lesson.
This lesson appears in the featured list.
No lesson with that title is mentioned in the article.
Templates & marketplace is one of the featured lessons.

questions.question: How can a user become a Notion Certified Admin?

options.text: By completing the Notion Academy certification path
options.explanation: The article states that the certification path lets you become a certified admin.
Certification is available for any plan type, not tied to a specific purchase.
Webinars help you learn, but certification requires completing the dedicated path.

options.text: By earning any badge in Notion Academy
options.explanation: Only the specific admin certification grants that title.

questions.question: Which path is described as “Start automating work, learn workspace architecture best practices, and harness Notion’s more complex capabilities”?

options.explanation: Essentials Path is for beginners, not advanced automation.
Workflows Path focuses on connected systems, but the description matches the Advanced Path.
The article’s description of the Advanced Path matches this wording.
There is no “Beginner Path” label; the beginner offering is the Essentials Path.

questions.question: What is the typical duration of the “Building basics” lesson?

options.explanation: The lesson listing shows a 20 min duration.
30 min is the length of the Custom Agents lesson, not Building basics.
15 min applies to several other lessons, but not Building basics.
40 min is the duration of the Templates & marketplace lesson.

questions.question: Which statement about Notion Academy’s live webinars is true?

options.text: They are only available to Enterprise customers
options.explanation: The article does not restrict webinars to any plan type.

options.text: They are on‑demand sessions designed to help users make the most out of Notion
options.explanation: The text describes live webinars and on‑demand sessions for skill‑building.

options.text: They require a paid subscription to access
options.explanation: No subscription requirement is mentioned for webinars.

options.text: They focus exclusively on advanced AI features
options.explanation: Webinars cover a broad range of topics, not just AI.

questions.question: Which learning path is best suited for someone who is new to Notion?

options.explanation: Advanced Path assumes prior knowledge.
Workflows Path is intermediate and focuses on connected systems.
The Essentials Path is described as “New to Notion?” and covers basic skills.
Custom Agents is a single lesson, not a beginner‑focused path.

questions.question: Which statement best captures how Custom Agents contribute to Notion’s workflow capabilities?

options.text: They automate repeat work and can run on a schedule or trigger, enhancing workflows
options.explanation: The article says Custom Agents automate repeat work, run on schedules or triggers, and integrate with existing tools.

options.text: They replace the need for any human collaboration
options.explanation: Automation assists humans; it does not eliminate collaboration.

options.text: They only function within the Templates & Marketplace
options.explanation: Custom Agents are independent of the marketplace and can work across tools.

options.text: They are only available to certified admins
options.explanation: The lesson is open to all learners; no admin restriction is mentioned.

questions.question: What are the primary benefits of earning a badge through Notion Academy?

options.text: Recognition of skill, ability to showcase capability, and learning at your own pace
options.explanation: The article emphasizes badges as a way to validate skills, showcase them, and learn on your own schedule.
Career advancement is not guaranteed by earning a badge.
Badges do not grant subscription benefits.

options.text: Direct access to Notion’s source code
options.explanation: Access to source code is unrelated to badge acquisition.
